cards.title: Vivian Vane
cards.notes: The Blue Canary's headline act — the **Nightingale**. Walked off after her last set on 3 October and never walked back on. Left a half-finished drink and a note nobody can read.

cards.title: Nick Marlowe
cards.notes: Private licence #4417. Hired by the club's bandleader to find Vivian — **quietly**, and before the papers do. Narrates this whole sorry business in the past tense.

cards.title: Dominic "Dutch" Sable
cards.notes: Owns the Canary on paper and the room in practice. Charming until the house lights come up. Keeps a detective on the force and an accountant nobody has met.

cards.title: Det. Roy Calloway
cards.notes: Homicide, 12th Precinct. First to the scene, first to call it a runaway. **Took a call from Sable at 11:58 the night she vanished** — then told everyone he was home asleep.

cards.title: The Blue Canary Club
cards.notes: Basement jazz joint on Vermouth Street. Cash in, cash out, and a back office that does more business than the bar.

cards.title: Meridian Holdings
cards.notes: A holding company with nothing to hold — a mail drop, a bank account, and a **ledger**. Every dollar the Canary can't explain runs through here first.

cards.title: The Meridian ledger
cards.notes: Eighty-eight pages of Meridian's "general ledger". The last real entry is dated the night before Vivian's set. **She'd been reading it.**

cards.title: Grausam closes the books
cards.notes: The books stop cold on 30 September — no closing balance, no signature, just a page torn at the corner.

cards.title: The Canary washes Meridian's cash
cards.notes: No club sells that many watered highballs. The takings and the ledger only balance if the difference is **somebody else's money going clean**.

cards.title: Vivian's final set
cards.notes: Half past eleven, 3 October. She sang *Nightingale* twice and left the stage between numbers. The club's own log has the times — **it happened, whatever it meant.**

cards.title: "Look in Meridian's books"
cards.notes: The last anyone heard from her — a text to Marlowe, timestamped 11:45, fifteen minutes before Sable rang the precinct.

message.body: If I don't ring by midnight, the answer's in Meridian's books. — V

cards.title: Vivian vanishes
cards.notes: By last call she was gone. Coat still on the hook, cab never called. Three staff put it inside the same half hour — **corroborated**, and impossible to un-know.

cards.title: Where is Vivian?
cards.notes: Runaway, hostage, or worse. The record doesn't settle it and neither does Marlowe. **Unresolved** — the honest grade for the thing at the centre of the board.

cards.title: You'll sing your last at the Canary
cards.notes: Sent to Vivian two nights before and cc'd — carelessly — to Meridian's accounts. Reads like a warning dressed as a love letter.

cards.title: Sable → Calloway, 11:58pm
cards.notes: Four minutes, precinct switchboard log. Placed from the club office phone to Calloway's desk **while Sable swears he was home in bed.**

cards.title: Sable's alibi: "home all night"
cards.notes: A signed statement, vouched for by Calloway. The switchboard log puts a four-minute call from the club office in the middle of it. **Refuted** — the record contradicts it outright.

cards.title: Calloway is on Sable's payroll
cards.notes: Sworn to by the bandleader, unproven. A cop who calls it a runaway before the coat's off the hook is either lazy or paid. **Asserted** — not yet decided.

cards.title: Sable's 1938 racketeering conviction
cards.notes: Nine years back a jury already found Sable ran a protection racket out of a supper club. **Adjudicated** — decided in open court, on the record.

cards.title: Sable conceded the club paid "protection"
cards.notes: In the 1938 consent decree Sable conceded the club made "security payments" — his own words, in writing. **Admitted** — established by the party himself.
